theme: one solver that must never meet its nemesis
title: One Circuit, No Nemesis
strategies: x-sort baseline: visit stops left to right by x-coordinate only, ignoring all other structure (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference)

single-recipe nearest-neighbor: always hop to the closest unvisited stop; a genuinely useful heuristic that nonetheless gets stranded on radiating spoke networks (commits to one spoke's tip, then pays a huge unavoidable jump back) and on winding corridors (nearest unvisited stop can be across the fold rather than along the path)

probe-then-dispatch: compute two cheap O(n log n)/O(n^2) structural probes from the raw points (coefficient of variation of nearest-neighbor distances; convex-hull vertex fraction), route to whichever of {nearest-neighbor, angular sweep, row-band boustrophedon} avoids that probe signature's known failure mode, then apply a shared bounded 2-opt polish that every branch reuses
exhaustive local search from any construction: seeded, deterministic 2-opt refinement passes on top of a chosen starting tour to squeeze out remaining crossings
